name: Duke Energy CORP
business: Duke Energy is an energy company operating primarily through regulated electric and natural gas utilities in the Southeast and Midwest regions of the U.S. It provides retail electric service to approximately 8.7 million customers and natural gas distribution to about 1.8 million customers. Its electric generation mix includes natural gas, nuclear, coal, and renewables.

exposures.sentence: EU&I owns approximately 55,713 MW of generation capacity.
Duke Energy owns, wholly or partially, 11 operating nuclear reactors located at six operating stations.

We are proud of the constructive regulatory outcomes that we advocated for our customers as we prepare for growth in energy demand driven by ongoing migration into our attractive service territories, continued electrification and onshoring from domestic industries, data center growth and other investments, including those related to support the broader utilization of AI.

catalysts.event: NRC issued subsequent license renewal for Oconee
catalysts.sentence: In March 2025, the NRC issued a subsequent license renewal for Oconee that allows an additional 20 years of operation through 2054.

catalysts.event: Duke Energy Progress filed subsequent license renewal application for Robinson
catalysts.sentence: Additionally, in April 2025, Duke Energy Progress filed a subsequent license renewal application for Robinson with the NRC to renew Robinson's operating license for an additional 20 years through 2050.

catalysts.event: Duke Energy Carolinas filed license application for Bad Creek Pumped Storage Hydroelectric Station
catalysts.sentence: In July 2025, Duke Energy Carolinas filed a license application with the FERC to extend the operating license for the Bad Creek Pumped Storage Hydroelectric Station.

catalysts.event: Duke Energy entered into investment agreement with Brookfield for $6 billion in exchange for 19.7% indirect investment in Duke Energy Florida
catalysts.sentence: In August 2025, Duke Energy entered into an investment agreement with an affiliate of Brookfield Super-Core Infrastructure Partners to receive $6 billion in exchange for an eventual anticipated 19.7% indirect investment in Duke Energy Florida.

catalysts.event: First closing of Brookfield investment expected
catalysts.sentence: The first closing is expected to occur in March 2026.

catalysts.event: Piedmont entered into purchase agreement to sell Tennessee business to Spire Inc.
catalysts.sentence: In July 2025, Piedmont entered into a purchase agreement with Spire Inc. for the sale of Piedmont's Tennessee business, which is included within the GU&I segment of Duke Energy and Piedmont.

catalysts.event: Expected completion of sale of Piedmont's Tennessee business
catalysts.sentence: Piedmont expects to complete the sale on March 31, 2026, subject to customary closing conditions, including approval from the TPUC.

catalysts.event: Duke Energy Progress received CPCN for second CC unit in Person County
catalysts.sentence: In October 2025, Duke Energy Progress received an order from the NCUC granting the CPCN for the second CC unit in Person County

catalysts.event: Duke Energy Indiana received CPCN for Cayuga CC project
catalysts.sentence: and Duke Energy Indiana received an order from the IURC granting the CPCN for the Cayuga CC project.

catalysts.event: Duke Energy Carolinas filed for CECPCN for new CC unit in Anderson County, SC
catalysts.sentence: Also in October 2025, Duke Energy Carolinas filed for a CECPCN with the PSCSC for a new CC unit in Anderson County, South Carolina.

catalysts.event: Duke Energy Carolinas filed for CPCN for two new CTs at Buck station
catalysts.sentence: In November 2025, Duke Energy Carolinas filed for a CPCN for two new CTs at the existing Buck CC station.

catalysts.event: Duke Energy Carolinas and Duke Energy Progress filed PBR applications in North Carolina
catalysts.sentence: In November, Duke Energy Carolinas and Duke Energy Progress filed PBR applications in North Carolina, which includes proposed cost recovery over a two-year MYRP period.

catalysts.event: Evidentiary hearings for NC PBR applications
catalysts.sentence: Evidentiary hearings are scheduled to commence in the third quarter of 2026.

catalysts.event: Duke Energy Kentucky natural gas base rate case effective
catalysts.sentence: new rates were effective in January 2026 for Duke Energy Kentucky.

catalysts.event: Duke Energy Progress South Carolina rates effective
catalysts.sentence: New rates were effective in February 2026 for Duke Energy Progress

catalysts.event: Duke Energy Carolinas South Carolina rates effective
catalysts.sentence: and will be effective in March 2026 for Duke Energy Carolinas.

catalysts.event: Filing to combine utilities in Carolinas
catalysts.sentence: In August 2025, we filed applications to combine our utilities that operate in the Carolinas by which Duke Energy Progress will merge into Duke Energy Carolinas.
We received FERC approval in January 2026

catalysts.event: Targeted effective date of Carolinas combination
catalysts.sentence: and the targeted effective date of the transaction is January 1, 2027

tamClaims.claim: Over the next decade, Duke Energy expects to deploy between $200 billion and $220 billion of capital into its regulated businesses.
tamClaims.sentence: Over the next decade, we expect to deploy between approximately $200 billion and $220 billion of capital into our regulated businesses.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 7,500 MW of new natural gas generation projects are under construction or seeking regulatory approval.
tamClaims.sentence: Our energy modernization investments are designed to ensure reliable and cost-effective energy while meeting expected growth in long-term energy demand and already include approximately 7,500 MW of new natural gas generation projects under construction or seeking regulatory approval across our service territories.
